title: Driver-race rolling state features
summary: State-space F1 tyre models treat pit stops as structural resets and lap times as evolving latent degradation; deep sequence models for pit decisions emphasize temporal dependencies.
rationale: State-space F1 tyre models treat pit stops as structural resets and lap times as evolving latent degradation; deep sequence models for pit decisions emphasize temporal dependencies. Current best features are mostly row-wise and do not explicitly use recent within-stint trends.
implementation_hint: Sort by Race, Year, Driver, LapNumber. Add lag1/lag2 and rolling 3-5 lap means/slopes for LapTime_Delta, Cumulative_Degradation, Position_Change, and TyreLife; reset rolling windows at Stint changes or PitStop=1. Include flags for sudden pace drop, degradation acceleration, and last-lap pit/out-lap anomalies.
expected_effect: May capture late-stint deterioration and strategy triggers missed by static TyreLife/degradation ratios.
risk: Rows may not form complete race sequences or train/test ids may be synthetic; ensure no future lags and compute train/test separately where needed.
